modules.description: Core pipeline agents: Paige (lead), Artie (architect), Una (UX), Sam (security), Beck (backend), Effie (frontend), Quinn (QA)
Code reviewer: Stan Standards (logic + performance + language-specific quality, read-only)
Ship agent: Devon Ops (docs + changelog + release + announcement, ultimate trump card)
Utility agent: Heracles (debugger, root cause analysis, on-demand)

Runtime evaluator: Val (test runner + live-app prober + verdict authority, separated from generator agents per Anthropic Labs harness paper 2026). Read-only — no Write/Edit. Cannot patch failing tests by design.
General engineering skills: c4-architecture, code-review, commit-work
Java source intel: canonical ripgrep + find + tree patterns for callers, impact, @Transactional scans, and call chains
Specialized pipeline skills: task breakdown, project discovery, post-deploy analytics, QA planning, entropy reduction, run profiling

SuperClaude knowledge-skills inlined from SuperClaude_Plugin v4.3.0 (MIT). Named-methodology skills only: spec-panel (10 expert personas), brainstorm (Socratic), research (adaptive). v2.6.0 dropped sc-pm (Paige's inline orchestration covers it) and sc-estimate (task-breakdown SP matrix duplicates it). Ships with ohmyclaude — no external dependency.
Sprint contracts (v3.0.0): write-contract format spec + calibration-examples + evaluator-tuning operational loop. Implements the negotiate-DOD-before-code pattern from Anthropic Labs' harness paper.
Common coding style rules — apply to all languages
Java rules — coding style, patterns, security, testing (path-activated on **/*.java)
TypeScript rules — coding style, patterns, security, testing (path-activated on **/*.ts and **/*.tsx)
Single entry point: /forge (route, sprint, release, commit, help)

Plugin-owned MCP servers declared in `.claude-plugin/.mcp.json`. Two servers: `ohmyclaude-fs` (filesystem helpers — exposes `tree` as a named, trackable MCP tool) and `ohmyclaude-probe` (runtime probes for @val-evaluator: http_probe full impl, db_state v3.0.0 stub). Both stdlib-only Node — no external dependencies. Approval prompt appears on first install.

Optional: `code-review-graph` MCP server (28 tools — tree-sitter structural graph, impact radius, semantic search, flow tracing, refactor preview). Upstream: tirth8205/code-review-graph (MIT, Python 3.10+). Re-adopts the graph backend flagged in ROADMAP near-term after the v2.4.0 cleanup. Runtime prerequisite: `uv` on PATH (auto-fetches the package on first invocation). Ships the MCP declaration in `.claude-plugin/.mcp.json` and a SessionStart setup hook that writes `.claude/ohmyclaude.local.yaml` with detection status. Real per-consumer opt-in is Claude Code's MCP approval prompt on first install.

Quality hooks: block writes with secrets (PreToolUse:Write|Edit|MultiEdit), run linter after bash (PostToolUse:Bash), inject calibration-examples.md anchor into val-evaluator Task spawns (PreToolUse:Task)

Tracking hooks (deregistered in v2.6.0 — scripts retained, registrations removed from hooks.json): backlog index rebuild, session summary, team cleanup. Pure observability; never changed agent behavior. Re-enable via custom settings.json registrations.
Cost-profiler hook: on SubagentStop/Stop, writes .claude/pipeline/PROFILE-<runId>.md and rolling baseline.json (measures /forge runs, pairs with skills/profile-run)

Per-project usage telemetry (deregistered in v2.6.0 — script retained for ablation campaigns, hooks.json registrations removed). Re-enable via custom settings.json. Was: SessionStart/UserPromptSubmit/PreToolUse/Stop registrations appending metadata events to <cwd>/.claude/usage/events.jsonl. Prompt bodies never logged. Pairs with scripts/usage-report.js for insight rollups.

Session intelligence hooks (deregistered in v2.6.0 — scripts retained, hooks.json registrations removed): was SessionStart /load hint, PreCompact stages.json snapshot, SubagentStart traces.jsonl. Pairs with /save and /load skills. Re-enable via custom settings.json registrations. Per-user preference; no longer in any profile.
Session lifecycle skills (v2.2.0): /save captures session state to ~/.claude/ohmyclaude/sessions/, /load reads and resumes it. Pairs with hooks-session. Host-local only — no repo writes.
User-invocable /save and /load commands for session lifecycle. Thin wrappers — real logic lives in skills-session.
